assertions.reason: Registration created the account and emitted a verification email, but the UI stayed on /register with an 'Account created!' confirmation card instead of redirecting to the onboarding flow.
Duplicate registration returned a specific enumeration-prone message — 'An account with this email already exists. Try signing in instead.' — rather than a generic non-revealing response.
evidence: ui-alert:An account with this email already exists. Try signing in instead.

assertions.reason: Submitting the weak password '123456' produced only the single client-side error 'Password must be at least 8 characters'; the validation surface did not specify the remaining uppercase/number/special-character requirements as the assertion requires.
evidence: ui-text:Password must be at least 8 characters

assertions.reason: Forgot-password for the registered account returned the generic confirmation 'If an account exists...' and emitted a real reset email in the shared dev-server log.
evidence: shared-log: To authreg-20260401@example.test, Subject 'Reset your password — Complete Care', POST /api/auth/forgot-password -> 200

assertions.reason: The valid reset token updated the password successfully — the old password was rejected and the new password logged in — but the app did not redirect to /login after reset; it stayed on the reset page with a success card and manual sign-in link.
evidence: shared-log: POST /api/auth/reset-password -> 200, POST /api/auth/login -> 401 then 200, GET /dashboard -> 307, GET /onboarding -> 200

assertions.reason: After expiring only this group's fresh reset token at the data boundary, submitting the real reset form showed 'This reset link has expired. Please request a new one.' with a 'Request a new link' action, and the server returned 410.

No real password-change UI was available while logged in. Signing in with the test account routed to /onboarding, which exposes no password fields or account settings, and navigating to /settings returned 404 instead of a password-change screen.

notes: Recovered the verification and password-reset links from the shared dev-server log as instructed.
The verification link for authreg-20260401@example.test was used successfully before later repeat visits showed the expected 'already used' invalid-link state.

Tried the assigned secondary email authreg-existing-20260401@example.test to obtain an alternate existing account for VAL-AUTH-022; the UI stayed generic but no reset email was emitted to the shared log, so no alternate seeded account was available within scope.
